name: Eight Bells Inn
description: 14th-century inn with flagstone floors, real ales, and hearty pub food. Originally built to house stonemasons working on St James's Church. Walkers warmly welcomed.

name: Badgers Hall Tearoom
description: Traditional Cotswold tearoom in a 15th-century building. Famous for homemade scones and afternoon tea.

name: Campden Coffee Company
description: Speciality coffee and light bites. Perfect for a caffeine boost before hitting the trail.

name: The Volunteer Inn
description: Friendly village local with a garden. Good selection of ales and simple bar snacks.

name: Noel Arms Hotel
description: Historic coaching inn offering comfortable rooms and a restaurant. A favourite overnight stop for day one walkers.

name: Public Water Tap (High Street)
description: Reported drinking water tap near the Market Hall. Availability may vary — check locally before relying on it.

name: Public Toilets (Sheep Street)
description: Free public toilets off the High Street, near the car park.

name: Campden Home & Garden
description: UNVERIFIED — this shop could not be confirmed to exist in Chipping Campden. Use the Co-op or other shops in town instead.

name: Broadway Tower Café
description: Café at Broadway Tower Country Park serving brunch, coffee, and homemade cakes. At the visitor centre car park entrance.

name: Water Tap (Tower Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap near the car park ticket machine. Seasonal — may be turned off in winter.

name: Toilets (Broadway Tower)
description: Public toilets at the Broadway Tower Country Park visitor centre car park.

name: The Crown & Trumpet
description: Traditional village pub with real ales, home-cooked food, and B&B rooms. Dog-friendly and boot-friendly.

name: Tisanes Tea Rooms
description: Charming tearoom in a half-timbered cottage. Renowned cream teas and light lunches.

name: Broadway Deli
description: Artisan deli with local cheeses, charcuterie, bread, and trail-ready picnic supplies.

name: Russell's Restaurant
description: Award-winning steak and seafood restaurant in a former furniture factory. Great for a celebratory meal after day one.

name: Public Toilets (Church Street)
description: Public toilets at Church Close Car Park, Broadway. Access via Church Close off the High Street.

name: Water Fountain (Village Green)
description: Drinking water fountain on the village green near the war memorial.

name: The Mount Inn
description: Cosy hilltop pub in Stanton with stunning views. Worth the short detour for a proper Cotswold pint.

name: Water Tap (Stanway Churchyard)
description: Outdoor tap in the churchyard. Refill your bottles in this peaceful spot.

name: The White Hart
description: 16th-century inn on the High Street with stone-flagged bar, real ales, and filling pub grub.

name: Juri's Tearoom
description: Family-run tearoom on North Street with homemade cakes and light lunches. A Winchcombe institution.

name: Winchcombe Village Shop
description: Well-stocked village shop with fresh bread, snacks, drinks, and basic camping supplies.

name: Public Toilets (Back Lane)
description: Free public toilets near the town centre car park.

name: Water Tap (Recreation Ground)
description: Outdoor drinking water tap at the recreation ground on Abbey Terrace.

name: Rising Sun Hotel
description: Hilltop hotel with bar, restaurant, and rooms. Panoramic views from the terrace — the perfect summit reward.

name: Water Tap (Golf Club Car Park)
description: Reported outdoor tap in the golf club car park. Availability unconfirmed — may be seasonal or restricted.

name: The Exmouth Arms
description: Traditional pub on Bath Road, Cheltenham — convenient refreshment stop before the climb to Leckhampton Hill.

name: Water Tap (Daisy Bank Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap at the Leckhampton Hill car park. Fill up before heading south.

name: The Falcon Inn
description: 16th-century coaching inn with flagstone floors. Real ales, seasonal food, and comfortable rooms.

name: St Michael's Bistro
description: Relaxed bistro in a converted church. Excellent local seasonal menu.

name: Olivas Delicatessen
description: Artisan deli with local cheeses, cured meats, fresh bread, and trail snacks.

name: Public Toilets (Stamages Lane)
description: Free public toilets on Stamages Lane car park, Painswick.

name: The Black Horse
description: Community-owned 17th-century pub in Cranham village, reopened May 2024. Good food and ales, walkers welcome.

name: Prinknash Abbey Café
description: Peaceful café at the Benedictine abbey. Homemade cakes and coffee in tranquil gardens.

name: The Old Crown
description: Award-winning micropub in Uley village. Craft ales and ciders in a tiny but characterful space.

name: Uley Community Stores
description: Community-run village shop with essential supplies, snacks, and cold drinks.

name: Water Point (Coaley Peak Picnic Area)
description: Reported seasonal outdoor tap at the Coaley Peak picnic area. May not always be available — do not rely on this as your only water source.

name: The Old Spot Inn
description: CAMRA award-winning pub with excellent ales and locally sourced food. Named after the Gloucestershire Old Spots pig.

name: Kingshill House Café
description: Community arts centre with occasional café and pop-up food nights (typically Thursdays). Daily café service may not always be available — check kingshill-house.org before visiting.

name: Public Toilets (May Lane)
description: Public toilets at May Lane car park in Dursley, opposite The Old Spot pub.

name: Water Fountain (Parsonage Street)
description: Drinking water fountain near the library.

name: The Star Inn
description: Traditional town pub with real ales, bar food, and a warm welcome for muddy walkers.

name: Wotton Coffee Shop
description: Friendly independent café serving speciality coffee, homemade cakes, and toasted sandwiches.
openingHours: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri, Sat: 10am-5pm (closed Wed & Sun)

name: Wotton Farm Shop
description: Local farm shop with fresh produce, bread, pies, and picnic supplies.

name: Public Toilets (Chipping Car Park)
description: Free public facilities at the main car park.

name: The Dog Inn
description: Welcoming village pub with hearty meals, local ales, and rooms. Popular with Cotswold Way walkers.

name: The Cross Hands
description: Country pub in nearby Tormarton with bar meals and accommodation. A handy stop before the final push.

name: The White Hart (CLOSED)
description: PERMANENTLY CLOSED since 2008. Now converted to residential use. Not available for walkers.

name: The Salamander
description: Bath pub with craft beers and stonebaked pizzas. A great spot to toast completing the Cotswold Way.

name: Landrace Bakery
description: Artisan bakery and café near the Abbey. Sourdough, pastries, and excellent coffee.

name: Good Day Café
description: Bright, friendly café serving brunch, lunch, and speciality coffee. Perfect post-trail refuel.

name: Bath YHA
description: Budget-friendly hostel in a beautiful Italianate mansion. Dorms and private rooms available.

name: Water Fountain (Abbey Churchyard)
description: Public drinking water fountain right at the trail's end point.

name: Public Toilets (Charlotte Street)
description: Public toilets at Charlotte Street Car Park, near the centre of Bath. 20p charge.

name: Water Tap (Stanway Cricket Ground)
description: Reported outdoor tap at the cricket pavilion. Unverified — may be seasonal or unavailable. Do not rely on this as your only water source.

name: Stanway Tea Room
description: Small tea room at Stanway House with very limited opening. Part of the Stanway House estate. Cream teas and light refreshments.

name: Water Point (Cleeve Common Car Park)
description: Seasonal outdoor tap at the main car park near the golf club.

name: Stream Crossing (Standish Wood)
description: Natural stream crossing on the trail. Not potable — use a filter or treat before drinking.

name: Old Sodbury Village Shop
description: Small community shop with snacks, cold drinks, and basic walking supplies.

name: Water Tap (St John's Church)
description: Reported outdoor tap in the churchyard. Availability unconfirmed — may be seasonal. Please use respectfully.

name: Water Tap (Holy Trinity Church)
description: Reported outdoor tap at the church gate. Unverified and seasonal — may be turned off. Do not rely on this as your only water source.

name: Cotswold House Hotel
description: Luxury boutique hotel on Chipping Campden's High Street. Spa, fine dining, and garden suites. Trail walker packages available.

name: The Chance (CLOSED as B&B)
description: The B&B formerly known as 'The Chance' has closed. The owner now operates 'Crafty Cottages' self-catering accommodation nearby. Check availability online.

name: The Broadway Hotel
description: 16th-century coaching inn with modern comforts. Central location on the village green with a well-regarded restaurant.

name: Crown & Trumpet Inn
description: 17th-century inn with real ale bar and comfortable rooms. Walkers especially welcome — boot cleaning station outside.

name: Winchcombe Camping
description: Small, friendly campsite on the edge of town. Hot showers, fire pits, and a 5-minute walk to pubs and shops.

name: Sudeley Hill Farm B&B
description: Working farm B&B with views of Sudeley Castle. Hearty farmhouse breakfast with local eggs and sausages.

name: The Royal Oak
description: Historic pub in the heart of Painswick. Real ales and home-cooked food. No longer offers accommodation.

name: Olde Dursley Hotel
description: Historic coaching inn in the town centre. Comfortable rooms and a restaurant serving local produce.

name: The Swan Hotel
description: Family-run hotel on the high street with a restaurant and bar. Good base for the Dursley to Old Sodbury section.

name: Manor Farm B&B
description: Cotswold stone farmhouse with views over the final ridge. Warm welcome and packed lunches available for the last day.

name: Russell's Fish & Chips
description: Award-winning fish and chips on Broadway High Street. Eat in or take away — perfect post-walk refuel.

name: The Plaisterers Arms
description: Quiet village local in Winchcombe with real ales, cider, and a small beer garden. Also offers B&B rooms. Less touristy than the High Street pubs.

name: The Bakers Arms
description: Friendly pub in Broad Campden with a pretty beer garden. Home-cooked food and North Cotswold/Wye Valley ales.

name: Co-op Food
description: Convenience store for last-minute supplies before heading south. Sandwiches, snacks, and trail essentials.

name: Co-op Food Broadway
description: Well-stocked convenience store on the High Street. Sandwiches, cold drinks, and basic first aid supplies.

name: Winchcombe Village Stores
description: Independent village shop with local produce, walking snacks, and a good selection of cold drinks.

name: Boots Pharmacy (Southgate Centre)
description: Pharmacy at 1 Newark Street, Southgate Centre, for blister plasters, painkillers, and first aid supplies. The trail walker's best friend.

name: Compass Inn Field Camping (DOES NOT EXIST)
description: NO CAMPING AVAILABLE. The Compass Inn is now a Best Western hotel with no camping facilities. For camping near Tormarton, check alternatives online.

name: Fish Hill Picnic Site Toilets
description: Public toilets at the Fish Hill picnic area on the A44, directly on the Cotswold Way.

name: Morris & Brown Café
description: Café at Broadway Tower Country Park serving brunch, coffee, and homemade cakes. Outdoor seating with views.

name: The Snowshill Arms
description: Village pub in nearby Snowshill serving real ales and hot food. About 1 mile off trail.

name: The Vine B&B
description: B&B in the picturesque village of Stanton, directly on the Cotswold Way.

name: Hayles Fruit Farm Campsite
description: Family-run farm campsite directly on the Cotswold Way near Hailes Abbey. Showers, toilets, fresh water.

name: Orchard Kitchen at Hayles Fruit Farm
description: Farm café and bakery serving teas, coffees, cakes and light lunches. Has public toilets and water refill. Right on the trail.

name: Postlip Hall Farm B&B
description: Working family farm B&B near Cleeve Common. En-suite rooms. Just off the Cotswold Way.

name: Co-op Food Winchcombe
description: Supermarket for trail supplies on Greet Road, GL54 5PU. The old North Street location closed August 2023.

name: Williams of Winchcombe
description: Excellent deli selling fresh bread, cheese, charcuterie and picnic supplies.

name: Cotswold Way Café
description: Café on the Cotswold Way at Cleeve Hill Golf Course. Panoramic valley views at over 1,000ft.

name: Cleeve Hill Hotel
description: AA 5-star boutique B&B on the B4632 at the highest point of the Cotswolds. Breakfast included; honesty bar. No restaurant — guests directed to local pubs for dinner.

name: Colgate Farm Campsite
description: Simple farm campsite 200m off the Cotswold Way near Dowdeswell Woods. No booking needed.

name: Crickley Hill Country Park Café
description: Café at Crickley Hill Country Park (operated by Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust) serving snacks and hot drinks. Public toilets on site.

name: Public Toilets (Crickley Hill)
description: Free public toilets at Crickley Hill Country Park visitor centre. On the Cotswold Way.

name: The Royal George Hotel
description: 18th-century pub with 34 bedrooms in Birdlip village. Traditional pub food, good value rooms.

name: Big Skies Glamping
description: Tent pitches in a wildflower meadow just south of Leckhampton Hill. Amenities block, fire pits.

name: Star Bistro
description: Café near Crickley Hill at National Star College. Breakfast, lunch and tea. Weekdays only.

name: The Painswick
description: Boutique hotel in a Palladian mansion in the heart of Painswick. Fine dining restaurant.

name: The Bell Inn, Selsley
description: 16th-century Cotswold inn with 2 AA Rosettes. Real ales from Uley brewery. Just off the trail on Selsley Common.

name: Co-op Food King's Stanley
description: Convenience supermarket in King's Stanley village for trail supplies.

name: The Village Inn
description: Village pub in King's Stanley (formerly The King's Head, renamed Autumn 2025 after refurbishment). Meals and real ales near the Stroudwater canal.

name: The Star Inn, Whiteshill
description: Dog-friendly pub with rooms. Home-cooked Greek cuisine and local ales.
openingHours: Wed-Sat: 12pm-3pm & 6pm-10pm, Sun: 12pm-4pm (verify locally — hours may have expanded)

name: The Rose & Crown, Nympsfield (TEMPORARILY CLOSED)
description: TEMPORARILY CLOSED since February 2023. Purchased by a community benefit society in January 2026; reopening planned no earlier than September 2026.

name: Public Toilets (Coaley Peak) — UNRELIABLE
description: WARNING: Multiple recent visitor reports state there are no permanent toilets at Coaley Peak car park. At best, occasional portable provision. Do not rely on this facility — plan accordingly.

name: Ashen Plains Campsite
description: Quiet woodland campsite about 1 mile off the Cotswold Way south of Dursley.

name: The Black Horse Inn
description: 16th-century village pub at the foot of Nibley Knoll. Indian and British food, B&B rooms.

name: Sainsbury's Dursley
description: Large supermarket on Castle Street in Dursley for trail supplies.

name: Co-op Food Wotton-under-Edge
description: Supermarket in Wotton town centre. Stock up here — facilities are sparse until Old Sodbury.

name: The Fox Inn, Hawkesbury Upton
description: Now primarily a tea room and B&B in Hawkesbury Upton. One of the few refreshment stops between Wotton and Old Sodbury.

name: The Beaufort Arms
description: Traditional village pub in Hawkesbury Upton. Short detour from the trail.

name: Hawkesbury Stores
description: Village shop and post office. Critical resupply point between Wotton and Old Sodbury. Open 7 days.

name: The Compass Inn (Best Western)
description: Best Western 3-star hotel with 26 en-suite rooms, full restaurant, bar, and event space in Tormarton between Old Sodbury and Cold Ashton.

name: Pennsylvania Garage Shop
description: Petrol station shop on the A46. Hot and cold food, drinks, and snacks.

name: Toghill House Farm B&B
description: Five-room B&B about 1 mile off the Cotswold Way near Cold Ashton.

name: Hill Farm B&B
description: Two-room B&B directly on the Cotswold Way at Cold Ashton.

name: Tesco Express Weston
description: Convenience store in Weston on the outskirts of Bath. First shop since Cold Ashton area.

name: Parsons Bakery, Weston
description: Bakery and café at 16 Brookside House, High Street, Weston. Fresh pastries, sandwiches and hot drinks.

name: Public Toilets (Victoria Park)
description: Public toilets (20p) in Royal Victoria Park, Bath. On the trail route approaching Bath Abbey.

name: The Raven
description: Historic pub near Bath Abbey. Award-winning pies and real ales. Perfect for celebrating completing the trail.

name: Bath Backpackers Hostel
description: Budget hostel in central Bath. Dorm beds and private rooms.

name: Water Tap (Standish Church)
description: Outdoor tap at Standish Church. Availability unconfirmed — may be seasonal or turned off.

name: The Vine Tree, Randwick
description: Village pub in Randwick near the Cotswold Way. Food and real ales.

name: Dale's Stores, Andoversford
description: Village shop in Andoversford with basic supplies, snacks, and drinks.

name: Cooper's Hill Car Park (NO TOILETS)
description: NO TOILET FACILITIES. Multiple visitor reports confirm there are no toilets at Cooper's Hill. Plan accordingly.

name: The Royal William, Cranham (CLOSED)
description: CLOSED. Put up for sale by Mitchells & Butlers. Currently closed with no confirmed reopening date.

name: Water Tap (Cranham Church)
description: Outdoor tap at Cranham Church. Availability unconfirmed — may be seasonal.

name: Stream Crossing (Kelston)
description: Natural stream crossing near Kelston. Not potable — use a filter or treat before drinking.

name: Ian Coley Sporting Café
description: Café near Seven Springs welcoming walkers with breakfast, lunches, cakes, and drinks. Outdoor gear shop on site. Toilets available.

name: Dunkertons Cider Deli
description: Organic cider taproom, bakery, farm shop, and deli at Dowdeswell Park. Pastries, bread, and artisan supplies. Toilets available.

name: Bath Soft Cheese Café
description: Award-winning artisan cheese café at Park Farm, Kelston. Cheese toasties, cheeseboards, salads, and homemade cakes. Indoor and outdoor seating.

name: Folly's End Farm Café
description: Farm café at Cold Ashton serving home-cooked food and full English breakfasts. Locally-sourced ingredients.

name: Café 25
description: Coffee shop on Weston High Street. Convenient stop before the final descent into Bath.

name: Beacon & Railway Hotel
description: Hotel, restaurant, and bar about 1 mile from the Cotswold Way near Haresfield. 11 rooms, locally-sourced cuisine, dog-friendly bar.

name: Cheltenham View Lodge
description: Self-contained holiday lodge on Leckhampton Hill sleeping 5, with panoramic views over Cheltenham. One shared Jack & Jill bathroom. Ideal overnight stop for Cotswold Way walkers.

name: Leckhampton Convenience Stores
description: Premier-branded village convenience store at the foot of Leckhampton Hill. Snacks, drinks, and basic supplies for resupply before the climb.

name: Cross Hands Hotel
description: 14th-century posting house with 21 en-suite rooms. Greene King inn with restaurant and bar. Breakfast available.

name: Windylands B&B
description: Family-run B&B on Tormarton Road between Old Sodbury and Tormarton. Three rooms with full breakfast. Popular with Cotswold Way walkers since 1987.

name: The Crown Inn, Tolldown
description: 16th-century Butcombe pub on the A46 near Dyrham. Food, real ales, and 9 boutique B&B rooms. Serves breakfast — a welcome early stop on the remote stretch south of the M4.

name: Water Tap (Fish Hill Picnic Area) — UNVERIFIED
description: UNVERIFIED: No online sources confirm a water tap at Fish Hill. One source states 'there is no water' here. Do not rely on this — carry sufficient water from Broadway.

name: Drinking Fountain (Winchcombe High Street) — UNVERIFIED
description: UNVERIFIED: The historical fountain at Abbey Terrace was demolished in 1959 and no modern replacement has been confirmed online. Use shops or cafes in Winchcombe for water instead.

name: Water Tap (North Nibley)
description: Outdoor water tap near the Cotswold Way in the North Nibley area. Availability may vary.

name: Water Tap (Dursley, near trail)
description: Public water tap near the Cotswold Way on the approach to Dursley.

name: Drinking Fountain (Kingsmead Square, Bath) — UNVERIFIED
description: UNVERIFIED: No evidence of a public drinking fountain currently in Kingsmead Square. Use nearby shops or cafes for water.

name: Woodchester Valley Vineyard
description: Award-winning vineyard and cellar door shop near the Cotswold Way. English wines, tastings, and vineyard tours available. The trail passes through the vines.
